title: teucrium townsendii subsp. affine
intro: Teucrium townsendii subsp. affine, commonly known as Mormon tea or sage of the mountains, is a plant species that belongs to the family Lamiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an expectorant, helping to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which may help to protect against oxidative stress and infections. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an herbal tea by infusing its leaves in hot water, and it can also be used in capsule or tincture form to take advantage of its potential health benefits.
